question: Compute exactly: 237 x 682. Give the exact integer.
How many strings of length 5 over the alphabet {a, b, c} have no two equal adjacent letters AND contain exactly 2 letters "a"?
How many integers from 1 to 1000 inclusive are divisible by 3 or 7, but NOT divisible by 13?
7 letters are placed randomly into 7 addressed envelopes, one per envelope. In how many arrangements do EXACTLY 2 letters end up in their correct envelopes?
What is the smallest positive integer leaving remainder 13 when divided by 17, remainder 7 when divided by 19, and remainder 2 when divided by 11?
Compute exactly: 599 x 129. Give the exact integer.
How many strings of length 5 over the alphabet {a, b, c} have no two equal adjacent letters AND contain exactly 2 letters "a"?
How many integers from 1 to 1000 inclusive are divisible by 11 or 13, but NOT divisible by 7?
7 letters are placed randomly into 7 addressed envelopes, one per envelope. In how many arrangements do EXACTLY 2 letters end up in their correct envelopes?
What is the smallest positive integer leaving remainder 6 when divided by 7, remainder 8 when divided by 17, and remainder 10 when divided by 11?
